name: Platinum Secured
effective_cost: Free — no annual fee, refundable deposit sets your credit line

verdict.text: Not a rewards card — use only if declined for Quicksilver Secured

earn_note: Like the unsecured Platinum, this card earns no cash back or points at all — it exists purely for credit access. If you want a secured Capital One card that actually earns rewards, use Quicksilver Secured (1.5% flat, same $0 annual fee) instead.

points.note: No points or cash-back program of any kind. The entire value proposition is the refundable security deposit and monthly reporting to all three credit bureaus, with a path to an unsecured Platinum card after demonstrated responsible use.

transfer_partners.highlight: No rewards currency exists on this card, so there is nothing to transfer.

protection_note: Bare-bones by design, even more so than Quicksilver Secured — Capital One's official Platinum Secured materials don't advertise purchase protection, extended warranty, price protection, or travel insurance. Treat this card as carrying no meaningful purchase or travel protections.
rental_note: No rental car coverage is advertised for this card — use a different card for rentals.

services.name: Refundable security deposit sets your credit line
services.detail: A $49, $99, or $200 minimum deposit — tiered by your creditworthiness at approval — opens the account with an initial credit line of at least $200. You can deposit more, up to a $1,000 maximum, to start with a higher line, and you have 35 days after approval to fund it in increments of at least $20.

services.name: Path to an unsecured card
services.detail: With on-time payments, Capital One may offer an upgrade to the unsecured Platinum card in as little as 6 months, at which point the deposit is refunded. The card itself carries no visible "secured" marking.

services.name: Automatic credit line review
services.detail: Capital One automatically considers cardholders for a higher credit line — with no additional deposit needed — based on payment history and creditworthiness.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: $0 on purchases made outside the US.

services.name: Credit bureau reporting
services.detail: Reports payment activity to all three major credit bureaus every month — the entire point of holding this card.

services.name: APR
services.detail: 28.99% variable on purchases and cash advances, with no introductory 0% period.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Can help build the authorized user's credit history too
No rewards earned — this card has no rewards program to share

additional_cards.note: Free to add, but since Platinum Secured itself earns nothing, the only upside for an authorized user is potential credit-history building, not rewards.
